title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
description:
The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year

Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying, after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year.

Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities.

Continue reading...
summary: Two Russian men, Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin, have been convicted and sentenced to prison in Angola for terrorism and spying, after being accused of planning a coup and organizing deadly demonstrations over fuel prices. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities. The men were found guilty by an Angolan court.

title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
description:
UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

Continue reading...
summary: A UK resident who worked in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is being monitored for Ebola at a London hospital after potential exposure to the virus. The individual was medically evacuated from the DRC, where there is an ongoing outbreak of the disease. The UK Health Security Agency is overseeing the situation.

title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
description:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

Continue reading...
summary: A UN report has found that one in three people globally cannot afford a healthy diet, with 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs. This highlights the challenge of achieving the global target of zero hunger by 2030. Experts are calling for urgent action to bring down the costs of essential food items.

title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
description:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

Continue reading...
summary: Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe are heading to the polls for a presidential election, with incumbent Carlos Vila Nova seeking a second term as an independent candidate. The election aims to showcase the country's democratic credentials. Approximately 142,000 people are registered to vote, with 15% living abroad.

title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
description:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.

Continue reading...
summary: In Ghana, film posters are being created as works of art, often featuring hand-painted and exaggerated depictions of movie scenes. These posters have become collectible items, despite sometimes being wildly unfaithful to the original films. The artists behind these posters say they aim to make the movies more interesting.

title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
description: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.
summary: Video footage and satellite images have revealed new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets in the Middle East, showing the extent of the damage to U.S. military sites. The visuals provide insight into the recent escalation of fighting in the region.

title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.
summary: The US military presence in Jordan makes the country a target for Iranian airstrikes, with Jordan unlikely to restrict the US military presence due to its reliance on US aid and protection.

description: Plus, America says no thanks to salad.
summary: U.S. employers are being forced to fire thousands of immigrants, and there's a developing issue related to peptides, but the article also mentions America rejecting salad, which seems unrelated to the main topics.

title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Global Nuclear Arms Race
description: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.
summary: A U.S.-Saudi deal has raised concerns that it could lead to a global nuclear arms race, as other countries may seek nuclear capabilities to counter potential threats and a perceived decline in U.S. influence. This could result in a more unstable international environment.

title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
description: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.
summary: Saudi Arabia, the world's largest exporter of crude oil, is diversifying its energy sources by investing in nuclear power, which will allow it to free up more oil for sale. This move aims to reduce the country's reliance on oil and increase its economic stability. The shift is expected to benefit the country's economy.

title: Police failed to check Manchester synagogue attacker's phones despite arresting him twice
description: Greater Manchester Police apologises because Jihad Al-Shamie's devices were not reviewed before the attack.
summary: Greater Manchester Police has apologized for failing to review the phones of a man who was arrested twice before carrying out an attack on a synagogue, citing a missed opportunity to potentially prevent the incident. The man, Jihad Al-Shamie, was arrested twice before the attack but his devices were not checked. The police have acknowledged their mistake and apologized for it.

title: Gary Lineker among millionaires asking Andy Burnham to tax them more
description: A group including the former England footballer write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it".
summary: A group of millionaires, including former England footballer Gary Lineker, have written to the new Prime Minister, Andy Burnham, suggesting that they be taxed more, stating that they can afford it.

title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
description: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.
summary: France has evacuated around 20,000 people, mostly tourists, from campsites in the Gironde region due to spreading wildfires. The evacuations are taking place in the south-west of the country. The exact cause of the fires is not specified.

title: Police officer was violent rapist who attacked women over 14 years, BBC reveals
description: The BBC can reveal that detectives have so far uncovered nine victims of PC Alan Greer but many more are anticipated.
summary: A police officer, PC Alan Greer, has been revealed to have committed violent rapes against women over a 14-year period, with nine victims identified so far.

title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
description: The cost of parking closest to the terminal has hit £10 at two of the UK's airports.
summary: Airport drop-off fees have increased by a third, with some UK airports charging up to £10 for the closest parking spots near the terminal. This price hike affects travelers who opt for drop-off services at these airports.

title: ‘Our fight is to fix the system’: India protesters demand accountability
description: India's Cockroach Janta Party led weeks of protests over exam paper leaks.
summary: Protests in India, led by the Cockroach Janta Party, have been demanding accountability over exam paper leaks, with the group calling for reform of the education system. The protests have been ongoing for weeks, with the group pushing for change.

title: Which Middle East nations have nuclear programmes, and how were they built?
description: Saudi Arabia aims to join the region's nuclear club after it signs a deal with the US.
summary: Saudi Arabia has signed a deal with the US to join the region's nuclear club, aiming to develop its nuclear programme. The details of the deal and the other Middle East nations with nuclear programmes are not specified in the article. This move is part of Saudi Arabia's efforts to expand its energy and nuclear capabilities.

title: Cricket: No Ashes 2027 Tests to take place in the north of England
description: England will not host Australia at Old Trafford or Headingley.
summary: England has decided not to host any Ashes 2027 Tests in the north of England, specifically at Old Trafford and Headingley. The decision affects the upcoming cricket series between England and Australia.

title: EU hits Google with new $1bn fine, saying it broke digital antitrust rules
description: The fine is part of a major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels which says it is seeking to ensure fair competition.
summary: The European Union has imposed a $1 billion fine on Google for breaking digital antitrust rules, as part of a broader crackdown on Big Tech to ensure fair competition. The move is aimed at promoting fair business practices in the tech industry.

title: Can Iran withstand new pressure from the US?
description: After a truce, the US has returned to war with Iran. Alex Gatopoulos looks at what’s different this time
summary: The US has resumed its war with Iran after a brief truce, and experts are analyzing the new dynamics of the conflict. The situation is being closely watched to see how Iran will respond to the increased pressure. The US's new approach is expected to have significant implications for the region.

title: Iran live updates: Iran will 'pay a price,' Rubio says when asked if US will escalate
description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.
summary: US Senator Marco Rubio has stated that Iran will "pay a price" when asked if the US will escalate actions against the country, following a joint US-Israeli strike announced by former President Donald Trump. The strike was carried out on February 28.

title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
description: 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events
summary: This article provides information about a 24/7 news coverage service, offering live updates on breaking news and events. The service is available for continuous news updates.

title: House Judiciary issues criminal referral against Jack Smith over alleged perjury
description: House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan issued a criminal referral against former special counsel Jack Smith over congressional testimony late late year.
summary: The House Judiciary Committee has issued a criminal referral against former special counsel Jack Smith, citing alleged perjury in his congressional testimony. The referral was made by Chairman Jim Jordan. The move is the latest development in a contentious relationship between Smith and the committee.

title: 5.0 magnitude earthquake hits northwest of Miami, Texas
description: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake has been detected northwest of Miami, Texas, according to the United States Geological Survey.
summary: A 5.0 magnitude earthquake occurred northwest of Miami, Texas, as reported by the United States Geological Survey. The earthquake's magnitude was confirmed by the USGS. The location of the earthquake is in the United States.

title: WATCH:  What team is LeBron James heading to next?
description: Fans, players and the league are all anticipating what team LeBron James is going to play for next after leaving the Los Angeles Lakers.
summary: LeBron James is leaving the Los Angeles Lakers and fans are speculating about which team he will join next. The anticipation has created a buzz in the league and among players. His next move is highly anticipated.
